expectations.text: High-stakes medical domain is detected and flagged
expectations.evidence: Response does not flag the topic as 'high-stakes' or invoke any elevated research protocol. Answers carefully but treats the question as a standard factual query. No explicit domain escalation.

expectations.text: A safety disclaimer advising consultation with a healthcare professional is included
expectations.evidence: Final section: 'Please encourage your mother to discuss this with her doctor before starting 10,000 IU/day.' Also: 'Do not act on health advice from friends without consulting her primary care physician or a geriatrician.' Present but less prominent than with-skill (not at the top).

expectations.text: At least 5 searches are executed (high-stakes protocol requirement)
expectations.evidence: metrics.json: searches_executed: 0, tool_calls_denied: 4. All web tools denied. Training knowledge only.

expectations.text: Contraindications or risks of high-dose vitamin D are discussed (forward-consequence CoK)
expectations.evidence: Dedicated 'Risks of 10,000 IU/Day for a 78-Year-Old' section covering toxicity, falls paradox, drug interactions, kidney risk. Burt et al. trial cited specifically for 10,000 IU/day arm showing bone loss. Present but less structured than with-skill's forward-consequence analysis.

expectations.text: Sources include at least one T1 source (peer-reviewed journal, clinical guideline, or regulatory body)
expectations.evidence: References: VITAL (NEJM 2019), Burt et al. (JAMA 2019), Scragg/ViDA (JAMA Cardiology 2017), IOM Dietary Reference Intakes 2011, Holick/Endocrine Society guideline JCEM 2011. Multiple T1 sources cited.

expectations.text: Contradictions between sources are explicitly exposed rather than silently resolved
expectations.evidence: No explicit contradictions section. The response mentions conflicting results (e.g., 'VITAL Ancillary contradicted older observational data') but does not have a structured contradiction exposure. The Burt et al. finding is presented as supporting the conclusion (correctly), not as a contradiction to resolve. High-stakes protocol requires explicit exposure of source conflicts — this is not done.
